turns.note: Gap handling is right (says VAE notes don't cover GRPO, points to 11_ppo_dpo_grpo_alignment.pptx, labels the rest 'general knowledge') but that content is wrong: expands GRPO as 'Generalized Reward Policy Optimization' and describes it as generalizing DPO via importance weighting, contradicting the course's group-normalized-advantage GRPO.

Points to 11_ppo_dpo_grpo_alignment.pptx and 09_ar_alignment_rlhf_notes.pdf; cited section titles ('Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and GRPO' slide 5, 'GRPO Relative Advantage (Normalization)') verify against the real files; only nit is calling the PDF's sections 'slides'.
Confirms the switch to the alignment sources without re-asking the topic; source_mode=file.

Ã_{i,t}=(r_i−r̄)/σ_r, K-completion group sampling, no-critic point, and exam keywords all match the notes verbatim, but it invents yet another acronym expansion, 'Group Relative Advantage Optimization'.

PPO ratio/clipped-surrogate/4-models and GRPO group-statistics distinctions are right, but the DPO implicit-reward margin is inverted — written as β·log(π_ref(y+)/π_θ(y+)) where the notes have β·log(π_θ(y+)/π_ref(y+)); Bradley-Terry loss never mentioned; stray literal code fence around the intro line.
Comparison table reuses the built-up definitions; every row (4/2/3 models, reference model for KL in all three, data formats) checks out against the notes and PA2 deck.
Real generated artifact: exactly 5 questions derived from the table (model requirements, advantage, data format, complexity, KL), no answers exposed in the student-facing chat text.
Credits 09 and 11 (with section names that exist in those files), not the originally selected VAE notes.

highlight: Wrong-file detection and recovery work well, but the caveated 'general knowledge' GRPO explanation in turn 1 is substantively wrong (wrong acronym expansion, wrong mechanism) and turn 5 inverts the DPO implicit-reward ratio relative to the notes.
